flashcards.definition: Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory; first detected gravitational waves on Sept 14, 2015.
First gravitational wave detection from a neutron star merger (2017); observed in light too — multi-messenger astronomy.
Earth-sized virtual radio telescope using VLBI; imaged M87* (2019) and Sgr A* (2022) black holes.
Combining gravitational waves, electromagnetic radiation, and neutrinos to study a single event.
Kepler/TESS/JWST have transformed exoplanet science from detection to atmospheric characterization.
